untranslatable_reason: Pair trading strategy requires two instruments and their spread calculation. The signal specifies 'spread between two instruments' but the backtest engine operates on single-instrument indicators only. zscore_20 is used as a proxy for mean-reversion detection, but this does not capture the true pair spread logic. The original source code snippet is incomplete MATLAB and does not clarify instrument pairing methodology.
translation_notes: Signal translated using zscore_20 as a statistical reversion proxy (1 SD entry ≈ zscore >1, 0.5 SD exit ≈ zscore <0.5). This assumes mean-reversion on a single series. For true pair trading, external pair construction and spread calculation are required before feeding into backtest engine. Sharpe ratios (train: 2.9, test: 2.1) suggest strategy is viable but backtest parameters (lookback_period, half_life) are author-silent and not encoded.
